hypothesis: Pretraining the multimodal contrastive model with SMILES that explicitly encode stereochemistry (chiral centers and cis/trans descriptors) will yield 3D embeddings that remain invariant to SO(3) rotations while substantially improving separation of enantiomeric pairs—measured as a >90% pairwise enantiomer discrimination rate versus the canonical-SMILES-trained model's <50%—thereby preventing mirror-image conflation without degrading rotation-robust retrieval.
